source_jobs.title: Senior Product Marketing Manager (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
At Upvest, we are on a mission to make investing as easy as spending money. Upvest empowers businesses to offer a wide range of investment products and the best experience in the field of capital market investment and retirement planning. Upvest’s Investment API is easy to integrate so that fintechs and financial institutions can save resources and fully focus on their core business.



We are proud to partner with Europe’s leading Fintechs and financial institutions such as DKB, Revolut, N26 and Raisin. Founded in 2017 by Martin Kassing, Upvest now brings together over 270 talented professionals from more than 70 nationalities. Upvest is backed by €280M in total funding from world-class investors, including BlackRock, Tencent, Sapphire Ventures, and Bessemer Venture Partners, Earlybird, Notion Capital, and Motive. Our latest €110M funding round in March 2026 - led by Sapphire and Tencent - serves as a massive catalyst for our growth, allowing us to offer premier investment experience.



ABOUT THE ROLE:

Our Marketing team ensures Upvest’s Investment API is widely recognised as the best on the market. We translate complex, regulated, and technical product features into crisp, differentiated messaging that excites clients and positions Upvest as the gold standard in investment infrastructure. We collaborate closely with Product, Sales, and Launch to drive growth, deliver high-impact campaigns, and build thought leadership.



As Senior Product Marketing Manager, you will own the go-to-market strategy for our product launches across Europe and the UK. You’ll craft compelling product messaging and positioning, develop enablement content for Sales and Partnerships, and showcase our product in interactive and accessible ways. Your role will directly influence how enterprise clients, fintechs, and the wider market perceive Upvest, ensuring our differentiation in a competitive landscape.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Own Upvest’s product messaging and positioning to make complex product features clear, compelling, and client-focused.

 - Own go-to-market comms plans for new product launches.

 - Create impactful product marketing materials to support growth across different ICP segments.

 - Drive product thought leadership initiatives and build Upvest’s product community through events.

 - Translate market and client insights into actionable input for Product and Growth teams.




WHAT YOU BRING:

 - 5+ years of product marketing experience in B2B SaaS, ideally within fintech, investments, or API-driven infrastructure.

 - Proven track record of translating complex, technical product features into clear, value-driven narratives across multiple ICPs.

 - Experience driving GTM strategies for product launches at scale-ups.

 - Strong collaboration skills with Product, Sales, and Marketing stakeholders.

 - Background in finance or investments.

 - Outcome-focused mindset: comfortable setting KPIs and measuring impact.

 - Hands-on ownership combined with strategic thinking.

 - Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to engage technical and non-technical audiences alike.




YOUR PLUS WOULD BE:

 - Have expertise with regulated products or multi-regional product market entry.

 - Have experience building and scaling product communities.

 - Are proactive in bringing the latest product marketing practices into your work.

 - Are active in sharing knowledge both internally and externally.

 - Speak German (C2)

 - Institutional Contracting: Lead and support the negotiation of complex B2B agreements with institutional counterparties, including credit institutions and global fintechs, across Upvest's offering.

 - Legal Ops & Automation: Drive the modernisation of Upvest's legal function. Champion the adoption of contract lifecycle management tools, AI-assisted review workflows, and scalable processes that free up capacity for high-value legal work.

 - Cross-functional Partnership: Act as a trusted adviser to Product, Engineering, Operations, and Compliance. Deliver clear, actionable guidance that enables commercial decisions cutting through complexity without creating bottlenecks

ABOUT THE ROLE:

Procurement at Upvest is currently fragmented — ownership is split across teams, there is no decision logic to route requests by risk or size, and no single person is accountable end-to-end. That changes with this hire.

As the first dedicated Procurement Manager at Upvest, you will move our vendor management from a decentralized model to a centralized, professional powerhouse. You will own the entire lifecycle of our partnerships - from initial sourcing and technical vetting to contract expiration management. Reporting to the Head of Network, you will bridge the gap between operational needs, financial discipline, and regulatory compliance.

This is not a process improvement project. What we need is permanent ownership: a single person accountable for end-to-end procurement, with the institutional knowledge and authority to drive outcomes.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Centralized Procurement Ownership: Act as the sole lead for all procurement activities at Upvest. You will manage the full lifecycle for Indirect Spend (SaaS, IT, Services) and provide strategic and administrative support for Direct Spend (Trading, Custody, Market Data).

 - Procurement Tool Product Owner: Act as the company-wide "go-to" person and administrator for our procurement tool. You will be responsible for its further setup, creating internal work instructions, and training stakeholders to ensure the tool is used effectively across the organization.

 - Policy & Process Architecture: Build and enhance Upvest’s procurement policies and processes. This includes implementing a risk-tiered decision tree (e.g. Legal review thresholds by contract value), a fast-track review path for expanded use cases of already-approved tools, and standardised business case templates to eliminate back-and-forth before the process can begin. You will create clear work instructions that ensure our procurement journey is transparent, audit-ready, and efficient.

 - Contract Lifecycle Management: Proactively manage contract expirations and notice periods through our procurement tool. You will partner with internal business owners well in advance of deadlines to ensure informed "renew vs. cancel" decisions.

 - Regulatory & Risk Guardrail: Work closely with our 2nd-line Outsourcing Management to ensure compliance with MaRisk/DORA/NIS2/KRITIS. While business owners hold primary responsibility for their specific tools, you will provide the oversight and structure to ensure all regulatory requirements are met.

 - Technical Sourcing: Leverage your understanding of cloud infrastructure (e.g., GCP) to challenge technical spend and negotiate favorable Service Level Agreements (SLAs).

 - Finance Partnership: Maintain a close collaboration with the Finance team to ensure all procurement activities align with company budgets and financial reporting.




WHAT YOU BRING:

 - Experience: 5+ years in procurement or strategic sourcing, ideally within a high-growth Fintech or a regulated financial

ABOUT THE ROLE:

As the Lead IT Risk Manager, you will play a pivotal role in owning and evolving our IT Risk Framework within the second-line risk function. Operating in a highly growth-oriented and regulated financial services environment, this role demands an exceptional blend of technical governance expertise, independent challenge capabilities, and strategic stakeholder management. You will serve as the primary second-line authority for IT risk matters, providing oversight to the first-line IT GRC team, leading comprehensive risk assessments, and ensuring strict alignment with Upvest's overarching Risk Appetite Framework.



WHAT YOU’LL DO:

Risk Framework Ownership & Oversight

 - Own and evolve the IT Risk and Business Continuity Management Framework within the second line, keeping it scalable as the business grows.

 - Provide independent second-line oversight and challenge to the first-line IT GRC team on the design and effectiveness of IT controls.

 - Lead IT risk identification, assessment, and mitigation across cyber, technology resilience, third-party, and data security, linking back to the Risk Appetite Framework.

IT Governance & Compliance Management

 - Mature the ISMS by guiding policies, standards, and procedures with the relevant process owners.

 - Define baseline controls and run continuous ISMS maturity assessments against ISO/IEC 27001:2022 and related standards.

 - Oversee third-party IT risk, internal technology exposures, and business continuity assessments.

IT Audit & 2nd Line Assurance

 - Drive second-line assurance reviews and deep-dives across critical IT risk domains, reporting findings and tracking remediation to closure.

 - Support internal and external audits, including IT General Controls (ITGC) and Application Controls.

 - Run preliminary internal IT audits to prepare engineering, product, and business teams for official engagements.

source_jobs.title: Corporate Tax Specialist/Steuerreferent (German) (m/w/d)
source_jobs.description:
Bei Upvest verfolgen wir die Mission, Investieren so einfach zu machen wie Geldausgeben. Upvest befähigt Unternehmen, eine breite Palette an Anlageprodukten anzubieten und ihren Kund:innen ein erstklassiges Erlebnis im Bereich Kapitalmarktinvestments und Altersvorsorge zu ermöglichen. Die Upvest Investment API ist einfach zu integrieren, sodass Fintechs und Finanzinstitute Ressourcen sparen und sich voll auf ihr Kerngeschäft konzentrieren können.

Wir sind stolz darauf, mit Europas führenden Fintechs und Finanzinstituten wie DKB, Revolut, N26 und Raisin zusammenzuarbeiten. Upvest wurde 2017 von Martin Kassing gegründet und vereint heute über 270 talentierte Fachkräfte aus mehr als 70 Nationen. Upvest wird durch ein Gesamtinvestment von 280 Mio. € von erstklassigen Investoren unterstützt, darunter BlackRock, Tencent, Sapphire Ventures sowie Bessemer Venture Partners, Earlybird, Notion Capital und Motive. Unsere jüngste Finanzierungsrunde über 110 Mio. € im März 2026 – angeführt von Sapphire und Tencent – dient als massiver Katalysator für unser Wachstum und ermöglicht es uns, ein erstklassiges Investment-Erlebnis zu bieten.

Als national beaufsichtigtes Wertpapierinstitut ist das Steuerwesen für uns ein zentraler Bestandteil unserer Geschäftstätigkeit. Es stellt die Einhaltung steuerlicher Vorschriften sicher, schafft Vertrauen bei Finanzbehörden, Investor:innen und Wirtschaftsprüfer:innen und bildet die Grundlage für nachhaltiges Wachstum – damit sich unsere Teams auf Kunden, Produkte und Innovation konzentrieren können.




DEINE MISSION:



Als Corporate Tax Specialist (m/w/d) bist du Teil unseres Finance-Teams und übernimmst eigenverantwortlich zentrale Aufgaben rund um Körperschaftsteuer, Umsatzsteuer, Lohnsteuer und Verrechnungspreise. Du sorgst für eine korrekte und fristgerechte Erstellung steuerlicher Meldungen und Erklärungen, unterstützt beim Aufbau robuster Prozesse und Kontrollen und trägst aktiv zur Weiterentwicklung unseres Tax Compliance Management Systems (TCMS) bei. Dabei arbeitest du eng mit Accounting, Legal, dem Head of Corporate Taxes und externen Berater:innen zusammen und bist erste Ansprechperson für steuerliche Fragestellungen im Tagesgeschäft.

Upvest befindet sich in einer Phase starker europäischer Expansion und gründet dazu neue Gesellschaften und Betriebsstätten im Ausland. Du begleitest diesen Wachstumskurs aus steuerlicher Sicht: Du bewertest die steuerlichen Auswirkungen neuer Länder- und Gesellschaftsstrukturen, koordinierst dich mit lokalen Berater:innen und sorgst dafür, dass unsere internationale Aufstellung von Anfang an steuerlich robust und skalierbar ist.

Wir suchen eine pragmatische, lösungsorientierte Persönlichkeit, die Verantwortung übernimmt, bestehende Prozesse hinterfragt und mit hoher Genauigkeit arbeitet.

 - Robuste interne Kontrollen für steuerliche Prozesse etablieren und laufend überwachen.

Upvest is backed by €280M in total funding from world-class investors, including BlackRock, Tencent, Sapphire Ventures, and Bessemer Venture Partners, Earlybird, Notion Capital, and Motive. Our latest €105M funding round in March 2026 - led by Sapphire and Tencent - serves as a massive catalyst for our growth, allowing us to offer premier investment experience.

As a nationally supervised investment firm, tax is a core part of how we operate. It ensures compliance with tax regulations, builds trust with tax authorities, investors, and auditors, and provides the foundation for sustainable growth - so our teams can focus on customers, products, and innovation.




YOUR MISSION:



As a Corporate Tax Specialist (f/m/d), you'll be part of our Finance team and take ownership of core tasks across corporate income tax, VAT, payroll tax, and transfer pricing. You'll ensure the accurate and timely preparation of tax filings and returns, help build robust processes and controls, and actively contribute to the ongoing development of our Tax Compliance Management System (TCMS). You'll work closely with Accounting, Legal, the Head of Corporate Taxes, and external advisers, and act as the first point of contact for tax-related questions in day-to-day business.

Upvest is in a phase of strong European expansion and is setting up new entities and branches abroad as part of this growth. You'll support this journey from a tax perspective: assessing the tax implications of new country and entity structures, coordinating with local advisers, and making sure our international setup is tax-robust and scalable from day one.

We're looking for a pragmatic, solution-oriented person who takes ownership, challenges existing processes, and works with a high level of accuracy.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Prepare and submit VAT pre-notifications, corporate income tax and trade tax returns, and other tax filings on time.

 - Contribute to preparing and maintaining transfer pricing documentation

 - Assess and support payroll tax matters in close coordination with the People team.

 - Develop and maintain our Tax Compliance Management System (TCMS) and related work instructions.

 - Establish robust internal controls for tax processes and monitor them on an ongoing basis.

 - Work closely with the Accounting team on tax matters during monthly, quarterly, and annual closings.

 - Contribute to cross-functional projects that align tax compliance with accounting and business goals.

 - Support the tax assessment and structuring involved in setting up new entities and branches abroad, including choice of legal form, registration requirements, and permanent establishment risks.

 - Coordinate local tax advisers in the relevant countries and ensure new entities are set up correctly from a tax perspective from day one and properly integrated into our group structure.

 


WHAT YOU BRING:

 - Several years of relevant experience in tax advisory, a corporate tax department, or audit with a focus on tax.

 - Solid knowledge of German tax law (e.g. AO, EStG, KStG, UStG) and a basic understanding of transfer pricing regulations.

 - Experience preparing tax returns and filings, and in dealing with tax authorities.

 - Experience with ERP systems (e.g. Oracle NetSuite) is a plus; strong Excel skills are required.
